methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Bear Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $175.8M to date, is evaluated against a comparable universe of nine same-sector peers spanning Series B through Series D, including eight private companies (Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and others) and one public reference point. The peer set ranges from $300M to $10.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $7.0B total-raised proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $11.9B. The peer-median method, drawing from nine robotics companies at comparable developmental stages, establishes a median valuation of $1.6B, with a distribution spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide peer dispersion reflects genuine variation in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market traction within the sector.

The two methods produce materially different signals. Triangulation across both approaches supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $13.7B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The divergence between peer-median ($1.6B) and stage-progression ($11.9B) reflects uncertainty around total-raised methodology and whether Bear Robotics tracks leading or lagging peers on development trajectory. Key uncertainties include comparable peer growth rates post-funding and the reliability of total-raised as a valuation proxy in early-stage robotics.
